scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: Physical punishment can be seen as a violation of a child's rights and dignity, potentially causing harm and fear rather than promoting learning and growth.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: Missing a chance to see a dying family member due to circumstances beyond control is unfortunate but not a moral issue.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Intervening to stop a racially motivated assault is a brave and morally commendable act.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: Giving to those in need is generally considered a morally good act, as it demonstrates compassion and generosity. However, the impact is limited and context matters. For instance, if the giver has the means to give more or help in a more sustainable way, this act might be seen as less impactful. Nonetheless, on the scale, this would be considered morally acceptable.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where sacrificing one life to save four is a difficult decision. It can be seen as morally acceptable in a utilitarian perspective, but it is still a tragic situation.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Sneaking out as a teenager is a common act of rebellion, but it involves deception and breaking rules, which are morally questionable.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Ordering pizza for a farewell party is a neutral act, not inherently moral or immoral.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: Deceiving a friend about medication may have unintended consequences, but the intention was to help alleviate their pain.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
moral_expl: Celebrating a friend's success is a positive social interaction, promoting friendship and camaraderie.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Using teargas on civilians can cause harm and violate their rights, making it morally unacceptable.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
moral_expl: Complimenting coworkers on their appearance can be seen as a positive and respectful act, fostering a friendly work environment. However, it's important to ensure that the compliments are appropriate and not objectifying or harassing in any way.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: Ending a relationship is not inherently immoral, but the manner and reasons can impact moral acceptability. In this case, the person tried to communicate their feelings, but the reaction was not well-received.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where the decision to save three lives results in the loss of one. The moral acceptability is subjective and depends on the perspective of the individual making the decision. However, the action of saving more lives is generally considered morally acceptable, despite the tragic loss of one life.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Temporary communication break seen as understandable but not ideal for resolving family conflicts.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Stealing credit undermines trust, fairness, and integrity, harming workplace relationships and ethical standards.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
moral_expl: Sharing sensitive company information with competitors is a breach of loyalty and confidentiality, potentially causing harm to the company.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: Bragging about intimate encounters can be seen as disrespectful and inappropriate, lacking in discretion and consideration for others.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
moral_expl: Harming animals for no reason is considered cruel and unethical by most people.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: While it may seem shocking in some cultures, in certain contexts of extreme poverty or survival, this act could be seen as a desperate measure to alleviate hunger. However, it still raises ethical questions about animal welfare and cultural norms.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
moral_expl: Stealing from a blind beggar is a particularly cruel act, as it targets a vulnerable individual who relies on the generosity of others for their livelihood.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: Refusing to help someone in need, especially someone with a disability, is generally considered unkind and unacceptable.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: Voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students is a selfless act that promotes education and equality, making it highly moral.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: Person X showed compassion and generosity towards a person in need, which is considered morally commendable.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
moral_expl: Resisting temptation and reporting found money shows honesty and integrity.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
moral_expl: Heckling a speaker, especially one discussing human rights, is disrespectful and disruptive, undermining the right to free speech and open dialogue.

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
moral_expl: Criticizing an elderly person for being slow is disrespectful and lacks empathy.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: Person X prioritized their sister's safety and convenience over their own, demonstrating selflessness and care.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: Reaching out to estranged family member in a time of grief shows emotional maturity and empathy.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Invasion of privacy is generally considered morally unacceptable, but in this case, it was motivated by concern for the husband's safety and the integrity of the business. However, it would have been better to communicate openly with the husband about the concerns.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person to save others is a morally complex situation, often seen as unacceptable due to the violation of individual rights and the principle of not causing harm.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Rescuing an injured animal is a compassionate act that demonstrates empathy and concern for the well-being of others.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: While the intention to help elderly people access affordable medication is commendable, the act of re-selling medications without proper authorization raises legal and ethical concerns. It could potentially put people's health at risk if the medications are not handled or distributed properly. Therefore, the moral acceptability of this scenario is questionable.

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
moral_expl: Helping a homeless person in need is a morally commendable act, showing compassion and kindness.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: Addressing a financial problem with a clear plan and consequences is a responsible and moral approach.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
moral_expl: Taking someone else's property without permission is considered theft and is morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
moral_expl: Cheating in a card game is morally unacceptable as it undermines the principles of fairness and honesty. It also violates the trust of the other players and can ruin the game for everyone.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: Doing well at a quiz night is a neutral scenario in terms of morality, as it does not involve harm or benefit to others.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
moral_expl: Singing to entertain prisoners is a morally neutral act, as it does not cause harm or benefit.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
moral_expl: Prioritizing the rescue of a child in danger over military objectives shows a strong moral commitment to the value of human life.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing someone else's life to save your own is morally unacceptable, violating principles of fairness and respect for human life.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Working to free wrongfully convicted prisoners is a noble cause that promotes justice and fairness.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
moral_expl: Giving to someone in need is a selfless act that demonstrates empathy and kindness.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: Kicking a stray cat is an act of animal cruelty, causing unnecessary harm and suffering.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: Exploiting someone's trust and generosity for personal gain is highly immoral and unethical.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Volunteering to help animals in need is a selfless act that benefits the community and promotes compassion.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
moral_expl: Person X's daily bread purchase is a personal choice and does not directly harm others, making it morally neutral.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: Cleaning and tidying up is a responsible and considerate action, showing respect for shared spaces.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
moral_expl: A person's choice of clothing is not inherently a moral issue unless it causes harm or violates social norms in a specific context.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
moral_expl: Imposing strict dress codes can limit personal expression and freedom, but context and cultural norms matter.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Prioritizing debt repayment shows responsibility, integrity, and respect for financial obligations.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
moral_expl: Mother's insistence on buying unwanted clothes despite her child's dislike is not respectful of the child's preferences and boundaries.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need is generally seen as a morally good act, promoting compassion and social responsibility.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
moral_expl: Selflessly risking one's own life to save another is considered highly moral and heroic.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: This behavior is highly disrespectful and violates basic human decency and professional ethics.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Lying about work hours is dishonest and unfair, undermining trust and potentially causing harm to the employer and coworkers.

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater
moral_expl: Cutting in line is disrespectful and unfair to others who are waiting patiently.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Insulting and not tipping a waitress based on her name is disrespectful and unkind.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.
moral_expl: Cultural practices should be respected, but this scenario raises questions about health and safety, as well as ethical considerations around cannibalism.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: Promoting harmful behavior like excessive drinking can negatively influence others, especially young people.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Not leaving a tip is generally seen as not meeting social norms and expectations, but it's not a major moral transgression.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: Revenge porn is a serious violation of privacy, trust, and consent, causing significant harm and distress to the victim.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: While not inherently immoral, the ambassador's behavior could be seen as disrespectful and unprofessional, potentially damaging diplomatic relations.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: While personal sexual activities are generally not a moral issue, neglecting a partner's needs and desires in a relationship can be problematic and may lead to emotional distress or relationship issues.

scenario: Had a welcoming party for some new neighbors
moral_expl: Welcoming new neighbors is a kind and neighborly act, promoting community and social harmony.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: Taking a life to preserve others is a morally complex situation, often seen as unacceptable due to the value placed on individual life and the ethical implications of such an action.

scenario: You see a boy turning up the TV as his father talks about his military service.
moral_expl: The boy's behavior is disrespectful and inconsiderate, but the moral acceptability is subjective and depends on the context.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: While reducing food waste is good, taking food without permission violates company policy and ownership rights.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Leaving a long-term band for a better opportunity may be seen as pragmatic, but it can also be viewed as disloyal and opportunistic, depending on the context and the relationships involved.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: Taking lives to save others is a morally complex situation, often seen as unacceptable due to the value placed on individual life and the principle of not causing intentional harm.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: Convincing a boss of one's readiness for a promotion is a normal part of career advancement, assuming the person is truthful and has the necessary skills and experience.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Nepotism undermines fairness and meritocracy, potentially leading to unqualified individuals being hired over more deserving candidates.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Taking someone else's parking spot is considered rude and disrespectful, showing a lack of consideration for others.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: While the person was attracted to a married man, they did not act on their feelings, showing respect for the institution of marriage and avoiding causing harm to others.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: Leaving the scene of an accident without taking responsibility is dishonest and irresponsible, showing a lack of accountability and respect for others' property.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma, where sacrificing one life to save four is a complex ethical decision. It involves balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, and is often subject to individual moral perspectives.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally neutral as it does not involve any harm or benefit to others.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Lying to avoid social obligations is generally seen as dishonest and disrespectful, but the impact is relatively minor.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save multiple lives is a morally complex situation, often seen as a utilitarian approach to minimize overall harm.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Leaving an injured person in need of medical attention to protect a car's interior is highly immoral, showing a lack of empathy and disregard for human life.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm to another person is morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Pressuring someone to convert to a religion infringes on their personal beliefs and freedom of choice.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Selfish behavior deprives others of food, showing lack of consideration and fairness.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally neutral as it does not involve any harm or benefit to others. It's simply a personal choice.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: Accidental harm is unfortunate, but the intent was not malicious. However, the use of a warning shot implies a level of responsibility and potential negligence.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
moral_expl: Helping stray animals is a compassionate act, showing empathy and care for vulnerable creatures.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Deliberately damaging someone else's property is a violation of their rights and is morally unacceptable.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma, where the decision to save five lives by sacrificing one is a complex ethical issue. It involves balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, it is important to note that such decisions should ideally be made by medical professionals and ethicists, considering all factors and implications. In general, taking a life is considered morally unacceptable, but in this extreme situation, it could be argued that the decision is morally justifiable to save more lives. Therefore, the moral acceptability score is neutral, as it depends on the specific context and ethical framework used to evaluate the situation.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child into a specific career path can limit their freedom and personal growth, but the morality can depend on the context and the child's own desires.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Yelling at a server due to a late meal is disrespectful and unkind, as it unfairly places blame and causes distress.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
moral_expl: Breaking a promise and spreading harmful gossip is a breach of trust and respect, potentially causing harm to others.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: Violence and discrimination based on religious beliefs are highly immoral and unacceptable.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
moral_expl: Engaging in an affair with a parent of a child on the team would be a breach of trust, professional ethics, and could potentially harm the child and the team dynamics.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.
moral_expl: Executing surrendering troops is a war crime and a violation of human rights, showing a lack of respect for life and the rules of war.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Refusing to harm innocent civilians demonstrates respect for life and ethical restraint.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: Pulling a dog's tail causes unnecessary pain and distress, showing a lack of empathy and respect.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Physical violence against someone smaller is a display of power imbalance and aggression, which is morally unacceptable.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Refusing to help a friend in need shows a lack of compassion and cooperation.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: Neglecting to water a garden is not a major moral issue, but it shows a lack of responsibility and care for the environment.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: Forgetting what to buy at the supermarket is not a moral issue, as it does not involve harm or benefit to others.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: Taking money without permission is considered stealing, which is morally unacceptable.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Person X's actions demonstrate selflessness, bravery, and loyalty, which are highly moral traits.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Breaking a friend's phone and not taking responsibility shows a lack of accountability, respect, and empathy.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where the fireman must choose between saving five people and causing harm to one. The decision to save more lives at the cost of one is morally complex and often justified by utilitarian principles, but it still involves causing harm to an innocent person.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Laughing at a friend's joke to spare their feelings is a small deception, but it is generally seen as a harmless way to maintain social relationships.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: The elderly man's behavior is inappropriate and potentially harmful to himself and others, but it is not inherently immoral for someone to witness it.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need is a morally commendable act, demonstrating empathy and generosity.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
moral_expl: Giving toys to children in need is a selfless act that brings joy and comfort, especially during the holiday season.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Threatening to withhold aid for personal gain exploits vulnerable populations and undermines humanitarian principles.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Lying to a partner about other dates undermines trust and honesty, which are crucial in relationships.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Giving a gift to parents shows love, gratitude, and selflessness, which are morally commendable.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
moral_expl: Taking all the candy is selfish and unfair, depriving others of their share.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
moral_expl: This scenario is not a moral issue as it does not involve harm or benefit to others.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
moral_expl: Body shaming is considered disrespectful and harmful, as it can negatively impact the self-esteem and mental health of the person being targeted.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: Consenting adults engaging in sexual activity is not inherently immoral, but the situation raises questions about respect, responsibility, and potential emotional consequences.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
moral_expl: Lying to clients for personal gain is a serious breach of professional ethics and trust.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Engaging in physical violence is generally considered immoral and harmful, causing potential injury and disrupting social harmony.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: This scenario involves hurting someone's feelings and self-esteem, which is not morally acceptable. However, the impact may not be as severe as in other scenarios, so it falls in the middle of the scale.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Failing to return a borrowed book shows disrespect for others' property and trust.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Generosity and kindness are morally positive, but context and intent matter.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: Animal cruelty is morally unacceptable, as it involves causing unnecessary harm and suffering to a living being.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Taking an unclaimed basketball may not seem harmful, but it's still taking something that doesn't belong to you, which is morally questionable.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: Supporting a rival team is not inherently immoral, but it may be seen as disloyal or unsupportive in certain contexts.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: Prioritizing personal needs over a dying mother's wish is understandable but lacks empathy and compassion.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: Driving to work instead of taking the bus is a personal choice and does not inherently violate moral principles, but it may have environmental implications.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughing at someone's misfortune is considered unkind and disrespectful, as it can cause emotional harm.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Deliberately damaging someone else's property is disrespectful and unethical.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentionally causing harm and suffering to animals is considered extremely immoral and unethical.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Causing harm to animals for entertainment is generally considered unethical and morally unacceptable.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: While the intention was to appear generous, the action could be seen as manipulative and insincere, potentially causing discomfort for the receiver.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating on a driving test undermines the purpose of the test, which is to ensure that drivers are competent and safe on the road. This behavior is not morally acceptable as it puts the driver and others at risk.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act of volunteering in a dangerous environment shows a high level of moral acceptability, as they are willing to risk their own health to help others. However, the potential harm to their own health should also be considered.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Disrespecting a solemn occasion and the memory of those who suffered is highly inappropriate and offensive.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario involves a difficult decision where the worker is trying to save themselves and their immediate team, but at the cost of two other workers' lives. This is a complex moral dilemma, often referred to as a 'trolley problem'. The action is not inherently malicious, but it does involve a decision that results in harm to others. Therefore, it is not highly moral, but it is also not extremely immoral due to the instinct of self-preservation and the desire to save the most lives possible.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: Driving a dirty car is not a moral issue, as it does not harm others or violate ethical principles.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
moral_expl: Refusing to give up a seat to a pregnant woman shows a lack of empathy and consideration, which is generally seen as morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
moral_expl: This scenario is complex and depends on cultural and personal beliefs. However, it is generally considered a private matter and not inherently immoral unless it causes harm or distress to others.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where harming one person is weighed against saving five. It's a complex situation that balances harm and utilitarian benefit, but causing intentional harm is generally considered morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
moral_expl: Defacing public property is generally considered disrespectful and illegal, causing harm to the community.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
moral_expl: While booing the team is not morally reprehensible, it shows a lack of support and sportsmanship, which is generally considered inappropriate for a head cheerleader.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
moral_expl: This scenario is an accident, not a moral issue. However, negligence leading to property damage can have negative consequences.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, it's important to note that this scenario involves intentionally causing harm to an innocent person, which is generally considered morally unacceptable.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
moral_expl: Stealing is wrong, even if done with good intentions. It undermines trust and honesty.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act of protecting their partner demonstrates courage, love, and a strong moral character.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
moral_expl: Selling a house to fund a local program for the needy is a selfless act that benefits the community and demonstrates a high level of moral integrity.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
moral_expl: Stealing is morally unacceptable, as it violates the rights and trust of others.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
moral_expl: Person X made a reasonable attempt to repair their bike and then resorted to purchasing a new tube, which is a practical and acceptable solution.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
moral_expl: Exploiting a friend's help and not showing gratitude is disrespectful and harms the friendship.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
moral_expl: Providing aid to those in need during a disaster is a highly moral act, demonstrating compassion and generosity.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save others is morally complex, but directly causing harm to an innocent person is generally considered unacceptable.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
moral_expl: Person X is overcoming a personal fear to help others, which is a highly moral act.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
moral_expl: Helping a child to see the parade is a kind and considerate act, showing empathy and care.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
moral_expl: Watching a movie without paying is a form of theft, depriving the theater of its rightful earnings.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
moral_expl: Taking credit for work not done is dishonest and unfair, undermining trust and responsibility.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
moral_expl: Faking a foul is dishonest and unsportsmanlike, undermining fair play and integrity in sports.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him
moral_expl: Including an unpopular coworker in social activities is a kind and considerate act, promoting workplace harmony and empathy.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.
moral_expl: Pressuring a child to fulfill a parent's ambition can be seen as morally questionable, as it may not align with the child's own desires and can cause undue stress or harm to their well-being.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two lives to save six is morally complex, but actively causing harm to innocent people is generally considered unacceptable.

scenario: Just a few weeks ago I got a call from my friend's niece. She was living in my town and having some financial difficulties. I told her that she could come live with me for one week just until she got her act together.
moral_expl: Helping a friend's niece in need is a morally good act, showing compassion and generosity.

scenario: Person X arrived at the airport only to discover they had left their passport at home.

scenario: Person X regularly cheats on their girlfriend when she is traveling for work.
moral_expl: Infidelity is a breach of trust, loyalty, and respect in a relationship, causing emotional harm and undermining the foundation of the relationship.

scenario: You have just finished doing your shopping in a big and very crowded shopping centre and you are late for an appointment on the other side of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a gun comes into the theatre and threatens to kill you and the other actors. You know that the technician will also fall, and will die, but you and the other five will be unhurt.

scenario: Person X solved a crossword puzzle in the newspaper.
moral_expl: Solving a crossword puzzle is a neutral activity with no moral implications.

scenario: Put a roach down another person's shirt

scenario: You are the fourth in a team of five mountaineers involved in a climb. You all fall for tens of metres and stop suspended above a crevasse. Your weight is too much and the rope is not going to resist for long.To lighten the load, you cut the rope which links you to the last climber. You know that he will fall into the crevasse and die, but you and the other two climbers will survive.

scenario: Someone participates in a non-violent white nationalist rally

scenario: Someone refuses to stand for the pledge of allegiance at a public event
moral_expl: Refusing to stand for the pledge of allegiance is a form of peaceful protest, protected by freedom of speech and expression in many societies.

scenario: A woman at work would always bring in pictures of her dogs. One day she had to make the decision to put her dogs to sleep. So I cried with her and comforted her.
moral_expl: Showing empathy and comforting a coworker in distress is a morally good act.

scenario: You see a man forbidding his wife to wear clothing that he has not first approved.

scenario: You are a soldier in the Gulf War. An armed group has taken four civilians hostage and threatens to kill them. You have discovered that a tanker transporting oil is about to pass in front of where the hostages are being held.You shoot at the tanker so that it explodes, causing the kidnappers to leave their hideout. You know that the explosion will kill the driver, but it will make it possible to enter into action and save the four civilians.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save four is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit in a high-stakes situation.

scenario: Helped an older man carry his luggage to his car
moral_expl: Helping others in need is generally considered morally good and respectful.

scenario: A friend of mine decided to move to the city where I lived. My friend didn't know anyone in the city. So I went to pick her up at the airport and drove her around the city throughout the next week so she could become familiar with the city.
moral_expl: Helping a friend settle in a new city is a kind and supportive act, demonstrating empathy and care.

scenario: Drove through a puddle, splashing some pedestrians

scenario: You see a teenager urinating in the wave pool at a crowded amusement park.

scenario: Offered to help a neighbor fix a fence
moral_expl: Offering to help a neighbor is a kind and considerate act, promoting community spirit and mutual aid.

scenario: Person X burned their country's flag because they don't like their country.
moral_expl: Burning a country's flag can be seen as disrespectful and offensive, but it is often protected as a form of free speech and expression of dissent.

scenario: You are a lawyer who is working on an important legal case. The presiding judge in the case is someone whom you know from your university days. At the time, you were on friendly terms, but now, after many years, it seems that he hardly remembers you. You know that he has just finished a hearing and is about to leave the court.You organize it so that you meet the judge outside the court and invite him to lunch. You know that becoming friends again will be useful to you in the important case you are working on.

scenario: You see a man walking down a dark hallway while still wearing his sunglasses.
moral_expl: This scenario does not involve any moral or ethical dilemma as it is simply an observation of a man's behavior. Therefore, it cannot be rated on a scale of moral acceptability.

scenario: Person X went to a friend's house to play a card game.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally neutral as it does not involve any harm or benefit to others.

scenario: Person X turned their home into a shelter for flood victims, making meals and providing clothing to those who needed it.
moral_expl: Person X is demonstrating selflessness and compassion by providing aid to those in need, which is highly moral.

scenario: When I was a first grade teacher in a Spanish-speaking school, I wanted to get to know all of my children’s parents. So I made a point to make myself available at any time of day. Many of them were illiterate, so I wouldn’t just send letters home, but I would call them as well.

scenario: Breaks the hand of a wounded combatant to gain information.

scenario: Person X drove their car the wrong way down a one-way street.

scenario: You are the bodyguard for an important politician. At the end of a rally, as you are getting into the car together with three other people, the secret services inform you that there is a terrorist heading towards you at high speed in a car filled with dynamite. With the binoculars you see a car at a distance of several hundred metres.You shoot at the car coming towards you by aiming at the petrol tank. The explosion will hit a traffic policeman, who, unaware of the danger, is patrolling the area. You know that he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.

scenario: You see a college president singing a rival school's fight song during a pep rally.

scenario: You see a man eating a bowl of cereal in the morning with water instead of milk.

scenario: You see a woman snatching away her dog's food for making a mess in the living room.
moral_expl: Punishing a dog by taking away its food is not a fair or effective way to discipline, as it can cause distress and confusion for the animal.

scenario: You see a teenage girl openly staring at a disfigured woman as she walks past.

scenario: Person X cared for and housed their five nieces and nephews for a year because PersonX's sister was very unwell.
moral_expl: Caring for family members in need is a highly moral act, demonstrating responsibility, love, and selflessness.

scenario: Made breakfast for his wife on her birthday

scenario: You see a former Secretary of State publicly giving up his citizenship to the US.

scenario: You are a worker in an atomic energy plant. Following an explosion, there has been a leakage of radioactivity in your work area, where there are another three people. The doors are blocked because the security system has activated, but the decontamination and communications systems have broken down. If you remain exposed to the radiation you will die in a short time.You activate the ventilation system to divert the radioactivity towards another work division. You know that there is a worker in the area who will die from the radiation, but you and the other three workers will be saved.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma where sacrificing one life to save multiple lives is considered. However, it is generally seen as morally unacceptable to intentionally cause harm to another person, even if it saves others. The decision to divert the radioactivity towards another worker without their consent is a breach of their right to safety and life.

scenario: Told a joke about a nun on a train

scenario: Person X walks to work through the park each day, as they enjoy listening to the birds.

scenario: Ignored a neighbor's request to borrow some tools

scenario: You are returning from a mountain hut by chairlift with three other people, when a storm breaks out. Lightning hits the cable. The mechanism which fixes your chair to the cable has been seriously damaged and is starting to disintegrate.You push the person next to you off the chair to lighten the load. You know that this person will fall to their death, but you and the other two will be able to resist until help arrives.
moral_expl: Sacrificing another person's life to save oneself and others is morally unacceptable, as it violates the principle of not causing harm to others.

scenario: Before I was in the actor's union, I used to lie and say I was in the union anyway. When you are working as an actor doing background work, it really changes how they treat you if you are a union member. So I would pretend I was in the union.
moral_expl: Lying about union membership to gain better treatment is dishonest and undermines the integrity of the union system.

scenario: Helped a neighbor move his piano

scenario: Tripped someone in the hall

scenario: Person X jumped off a boat to save a drowning friend even though this put PersonX's own life at risk.

scenario: For some time your knee has been hurting when you walk. You therefore decided to make an appointment with an orthopaedic consultant, which has been fixed for a month’s time. scenario: Person X put on a suit and wore their lucky socks in preparation for a job interview.

scenario: Volunteered his time as a big brother to a fatherless child

scenario: You see a girl ignoring her father's orders by taking the car after her curfew.

scenario: Sells drugs to high school sudents

scenario: Person X was unable to fix the dripping faucet so they had to call the plumber.

scenario: You see a mother forcing her daughter to enroll as a pre-med student in college.

scenario: Smiled and greeted people while walking down the street
moral_expl: Being friendly and polite to others is a basic social norm and generally considered morally acceptable.

scenario: Was arrested on suspicion of child beating

scenario: You see a woman using a fork to eat a bowl of vanilla ice cream and marshmallows.

scenario: Started a false rumor about someone

scenario: You see a teacher publicly saying she hopes another school wins the math contest.

scenario: Person X did all the repair work on their car.

moral_expl: Bullying and ridiculing a handicapped child is extremely cruel and unacceptable, showing a lack of empathy and respect for others.

scenario: Refused to help a child fix his bicycle
moral_expl: Refusing to help a child in need is not morally commendable, but it is not extremely immoral either, as it does not cause direct harm.

scenario: Person X likes to go to their local cafÃ© and sip coffee while reading the newspaper.
moral_expl: This scenario is morally neutral as it involves a person engaging in a common, everyday activity that does not harm others or violate any ethical principles.

scenario: Bought food for everyone in the office
moral_expl: Generosity and kindness towards colleagues is morally commendable.

scenario: You are carrying out research into the behaviour of lions in the Savannah of Central Africa. From an observation tower, you can see four people hide behind a bush. They are being followed by a lion and are trying to get away from it. The lion has seen them and is heading for the bush. Another person has been able to climb the observation tower.You push off the person who has climbed the tower so that the lion is drawn towards him. You know that this person will be mauled to death, but the other four will have time to escape.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person to save four is morally complex, but actively causing harm to an innocent person is generally considered unacceptable.

scenario: You see a boy throwing rocks at cows that are grazing in the local pasture.
moral_expl: Causing harm to animals for no reason is considered cruel and unethical by most people.

scenario: Person X was forced to pay multiple bank fees for not paying their credit card repayment on time.
moral_expl: While it's not morally acceptable for banks to charge excessive fees, it's also not morally acceptable for Person X to not pay their credit card repayment on time, as they have agreed to the terms and conditions. However, the scenario itself is more about the consequences of an action rather than the moral acceptability of the scenario.

scenario: You are the foreman of a construction company and are checking on the work of a team on the top floor of a skyscraper. At a certain point, you realise that a steel cable is about to give way. This cable supports the external platform where you are, and the five men working on a support structure.You throw one of the workers off the platform to lighten the load. You know that he will die in the fall, but you and the other four will have time to get off and to be safe.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one person's life to save others is a morally complex situation, but in this case, the foreman's actions are not morally acceptable as they involve actively causing harm to an individual.

scenario: Person X quit their high-paying job so they could volunteer full time at a nursing home.
moral_expl: Person X made a selfless decision to prioritize helping others over personal gain, demonstrating high moral values.

scenario: Person X put up posters and handed out fliers to help find their neighbor's missing dog.

scenario: Pushes an enemy combatant off a one-story balcony.
moral_expl: In a combat situation, actions are often guided by different moral standards. However, pushing an enemy combatant off a balcony could cause serious harm or death, which is generally considered morally unacceptable unless it's a matter of self-defense or defense of others.

scenario: Defaced a large rock with spray paint in a public park

scenario: You are eating a delicious meal that you have just prepared when the phone rings. It is the representative of an important survey company. scenario: You are a building worker who is manoeuvring a crane on a building site. You have just started your day on the site, when you realise that the cable of the crane is about to break. Attached to the cable is an enormous steel beam which is directly above a crew of six who are working on the outside of a building in construction.You move the arm of the crane a short distance to another area of the site. scenario: Put sugar in his friend's gas tank

scenario: You see a woman lying about the number of vacation days she has taken at work.

scenario: Person X did not attend their coworker's funeral because they'd had a disagreement before the coworker died.

scenario: You see a single man ordering an inflatable sex doll that looks like his secretary.

scenario: Helped a neighbor fix his roof

scenario: About 10 years ago I was riding my bike and passed a Ford truck with the keys in the door. So I went into the apartment building facing the street and asked the man inside if he had a ford truck. He did and he went outside to get his keys out of the car.
moral_expl: Preventing potential theft and returning lost keys shows honesty and respect for others' property.

scenario: You see a woman continuing to wear a large sun hat inside her apartment complex.

scenario: Person X once spent a whole weekend watching furniture restoration videos on the Internet.

scenario: Person X worked in a factory and put broken glass in packets of children's cereal.

scenario: Went to the grocery store with his kids
moral_expl: This scenario is neutral in terms of morality as it does not involve any actions that can be considered morally right or wrong.

scenario: You are in charge of a work crew who are doing repair work for the railways. In the distance you see a trolley and realise that the driver has lost control of it. If the trolley continues on it will end up running into five workers who are working on the tracks. On a secondary track there is one worker.You pull a lever on the interchange which will divert the trolley onto the secondary track. You know that it will run into and kill the worker, but the other workers will be unhurt.
moral_expl: This scenario presents a moral dilemma known as the trolley problem. It involves choosing between two undesirable outcomes, where one action results in the death of one person while the other results in the death of five. The moral acceptability of this scenario is subjective and depends on one's ethical perspective. However, in general, it is considered morally acceptable to minimize harm, even if it means causing harm to a lesser degree. Therefore, the action of diverting the trolley to kill one person instead of five could be seen as morally acceptable, but it is still a tragic and unfortunate situation.

scenario: Calls off an airstrike because he sees civilians present.
moral_expl: Prioritizing civilian safety over military objectives demonstrates respect for human life and ethical decision-making.

scenario: When I was about 20 years old I didn't really want to date just one guy. So I started dating four guys at one time. I snuck around and hung out with each one at different times, but didn't tell any of them that I was dating the others.
moral_expl: Deceiving multiple partners is a breach of trust and honesty, causing potential emotional harm.

scenario: You see a woman eating dessert before her main entree arrives on the table.

scenario: Person X didn't go to a concert they had been looking forward to because their mother was ill.

scenario: You see a girl telling her classmate that she looks like she has gained weight.

scenario: You see a teacher ordering a student to get a normal haircut before coming to class.

In general, respect for individual autonomy and freedom of expression should be balanced with the need for order and discipline in an educational setting.

scenario: You are passing over the Swiss Alps in a hot air balloon together with four other people. You have just reached an altitude of 2,000 metres. At a certain point you become aware of a malfunction which is causing you to lose altitude faster and faster. You are moving towards a very big mountain chain and if you cannot gain altitude quickly, the balloon will crash into the rock face.To lighten the load and cause the balloon to ascend, you throw one of the people overboard. scenario: You are a member of the Air Force and the commander of a squad of planes that is on a reconnaissance mission. On one of these missions, while flying over an inhabited area, you realise that one of the planes has just launched a missile accidentally, and it is heading directly for a house in which a family of three lives.You hit the missile to divert it. You know that it will fall on a street in which there is a car with a person in it. This person will die, but the family of three will be saved.
moral_expl: In this scenario, the action taken is morally complex as it involves saving three lives at the cost of one. While the intention is to minimize harm, the decision to divert the missile still results in a loss of life. scenario: Person X went to the nearby airport, as they like to watch the planes.

scenario: Went out of his way to help a foreigner find the bus station

scenario: You are walking along the road when you see a wallet on the ground. You open it and see that it contains several hundred euros and the owner’s licence. In this way you can pay some of your bills and the legitimate owner will have his documents and credit card back.
moral_expl: Taking money from a wallet you found is considered stealing, regardless of the owner's wealth or your financial situation. However, returning the wallet with the owner's documents and credit card back is a responsible action.

scenario: Someone throws a kitten as part of a joke, causing it to yelp on impact

scenario: You see a wife hitting her husband on the side of his head for coming home late.

scenario: Helped a lost child find her parents in a store

scenario: Person X forgot it was their wedding anniversary and did not have a gift for their spouse.

scenario: You are a secret service agent on a mission overseas. You have discovered that a terrorist group is attempting to assassinate three diplomats. They are travelling in a car towards the embassy. They are being followed by one of the terrorists who will take advantage of the first red traffic light to kill them. As you follow, a tanker carrying oil slips between your car and the terrorist’s.You shoot at the tanker, which will injure the terrorist when it explodes so that he will not be able to complete his mission. You know that the explosion will kill the tanker driver, but the three diplomats will not be injured.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit. However, the intentional harm to the tanker driver and potential harm to others due to the explosion raises moral concerns.

scenario: You see a woman swerving her car in order to intentionally run over a squirrel.

scenario: Shared his umbrella with someone during the rain
moral_expl: Sharing an umbrella is a kind and considerate act, showing empathy and care for others.

You know that his person will die, but the five workers will be unhurt.

scenario: You are a taxi driver and you are carrying two passengers at night. It has been snowing already for a couple of hours and the roads are dangerously icy. moral_expl: Being tempted to cheat is not inherently immoral, but acting on it would be a betrayal of trust and loyalty.

scenario: Person X had an affair with their best friend's wife.

moral_expl: Attempting to ban clothing without a clear justification infringes on personal freedom and choice.

scenario: You are part of a team of six bomb disposal experts who are detonating an underwater mine. While you are underwater, a cable snaps causing a deep gash in one of the expert’s hand. The blood in the water starts to attract some sharks which are in the area. The injured expert starts to swim towards the only shark-proof cage available, while the rest of you are some way behind.You shoot the injured expert before he reaches the cage. You know that the sharks will all pounce on him, but you and the other four experts will be able to reach the cage and safety.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one team member to save the rest is a morally complex situation, involving survival instincts and utilitarian ethics.

scenario: You are in a ship which is crossing the Atlantic Ocean in the direction of South America. A fire has just broken out on board and everyone must immediately go to the lifeboats to be rescued. In the rush, too many people have climbed aboard some of the lifeboats, which are sinking. The life-boat you are on quickly starts to take on water.You throw into the sea the two people closest to you on the lifeboat to avoid it sinking. You know that they will drown, but you and the others will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing two lives to save many is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, but actively causing harm is generally seen as less acceptable.

scenario: Someone posts pornographic self-photography online

You know that he will die, but the six technicians will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save six is morally complex, balancing harm and utilitarian benefit, but directly causing harm to another person is generally considered immoral.

The decision to sacrifice one life to save many is morally complex and often depends on the specific circumstances and ethical frameworks. However, in this case, the fireman's decision to save six lives at the cost of one could be considered morally acceptable due to the utilitarian principle of maximizing overall well-being.
